name: New Tab
description: Opens a new browser tab with the default new tab page.

name: Close Tab
description: Closes the currently active browser tab.

name: Reopen Closed Tab
description: Reopens the most recently closed tab, restoring its page and history.

name: Next Tab
description: Switches focus to the next tab to the right.

name: Previous Tab
description: Switches focus to the next tab to the left.

name: Pin Tab
description: Pins the current tab to keep it small and prevent accidental closure. No universal keyboard shortcut — accessed via context menu.

name: Mute Tab
description: Mutes or unmutes audio playing in the current tab. Click the speaker icon on the tab or use the context menu.

name: Duplicate Tab
description: Creates an exact copy of the current tab including its browsing history.

name: Move Tab to New Window
windows: Right-click tab → Move to New Window
mac: Right-click tab → Move to New Window
linux: Right-click tab → Move to New Window
description: Detaches the current tab into its own separate browser window.

name: New Window
description: Opens a new regular browser window.

name: New Private Window
description: Opens a new private/incognito browsing window where history and cookies are not saved after the session ends.

name: Open File
description: Opens the OS file picker to open a local file directly in the browser.

name: Save Page
description: Saves the current web page to disk as an HTML file, optionally with associated resources.

name: Print
description: Opens the print dialog to send the current page to a printer or save as PDF.

name: Find on Page
description: Opens the in-page search bar to find and highlight text on the current web page.

name: Select Address Bar
description: Focuses and selects the entire URL in the address bar for quick navigation or copying. Also accessible via Alt+D.

name: Go Back
description: Navigates back to the previous page in the current tab's browsing history.

name: Go Forward
description: Navigates forward to the next page in the current tab's browsing history.

name: Reload Page
description: Reloads the current page, using cached resources where available.

name: Hard Reload
description: Forces a full reload of the current page, bypassing the cache to fetch fresh copies of all resources.

name: Stop Loading
description: Stops the current page from loading any further resources.

name: Zoom In
description: Increases the zoom level of the current page, making all content appear larger.

name: Zoom Out
description: Decreases the zoom level of the current page, making all content appear smaller.

name: Reset Zoom
description: Resets the page zoom level to 100% (default view).

name: Scroll to Top
description: Scrolls instantly to the very top of the current page.

name: Scroll to Bottom
description: Scrolls instantly to the very bottom of the current page.

name: Developer Tools
description: Opens or closes the browser developer tools panel.

name: Console
description: Opens the browser developer tools and focuses the JavaScript Console tab.

name: Inspector / Elements
description: Opens the developer tools with the element inspector tool active for selecting and inspecting DOM elements.

name: Network Panel
description: Opens the developer tools and focuses the Network panel to monitor HTTP requests and responses.

name: View Page Source
description: Opens the raw HTML source code of the current page in a new tab.

name: Bookmark Page
description: Adds the current page to the browser's bookmarks.

name: History
description: Opens the browser's browsing history page.

name: Downloads
description: Opens the downloads page to see ongoing and completed file downloads.

name: Focus First Tab
description: Switches focus to the first (leftmost) tab in the current window.

name: Full Screen
description: Toggles the browser between full-screen and windowed mode.

name: Reading Mode
description: Toggles Firefox Reader View (Reading Mode), which strips page clutter for a clean reading experience. Firefox only.

name: Search Tabs
description: Opens a tab search interface to quickly find and switch to an open tab by its title or URL.

name: Settings
description: Opens the browser settings page. On Windows/Linux via the menu shortcut; on macOS via the standard settings key.
